hypothesis: Training 3D molecular generative models with corrected aromatic valency handling and chemically accurate (aromatic-dependent or Kekulized) datasets will reduce valency errors and produce generated structures with significantly improved agreement to GFN2-xTB-optimized geometries (lower Δr, Δθ, Δφ, and ΔE_relax) compared to models trained on the original GEOM-Drugs data, while MMFF94-based evaluations will substantially overestimate geometric relaxation energies relative to GFN2-xTB.
